rulings.Case Title: BURNS TOMMY et al VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 4, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. The allegations raised in the third amended complaint were decided in Strauss vs. Horton (2009) 46 Cal.4th 364. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LARION KRAYZMAN VS. JULIE E LAKE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 4, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT JULIE LAKE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied, triable issues of material fact raised: 1) Whether plaintiff has standing to maintain this action and is a proper party plaintiff. (Krayzman declaration, Exhibits 1 & 2.) 2) Whether an agreement was formed based on prior transactions between the parties that the account terms are true and the balance is due and owing. (Krayzman declaration, Exhibit 4, 5, and 6.) 3) Whether the assignee of the account is Federal credit Corp. or Crayzman. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARLEANNE LEGASPI et al VS. ELBERT S ACOSTA SR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action (Ccp 405.23, 405.30, 405.31 And 405.32
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 4, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANTS ELBERT ACOSTA, and, FELY ACOSTA Motion to Expunge Notice of Pendency of Action (CCP 405.23, 405.30, 405.31 and 405.32). Motion to expunge granted. Ruling is not based on technical violation of CCP section 405.23, defendants had actual notice of lis pendens. In view of the declaratory relief and injunction to restore plaintiffs' possession of the premises, the claim relates to real property. Plaintiffs have not carried their burden to establish by a preponderance of the evidence the probable validity of their claim to the premises. Defendants present evidence that the reason they could not make repairs was because they had no money to do so, and their listing the building for sale supports this. Further, evidence is presented that the buyers are willing to repair the fire damage and re-lease plaintiffs apartment to them. Request for attorney fees denied, plaintiffs acted with substantial justification. CCP section 405.38. =(302/CWW)
